body.title: /review build-tightness grade: could a cheap builder execute this?
body.result: /review SKILL.md Check 8: build-tightness grade (tight/medium/loose) from 4 structured fields. Loose forces AMEND with bounce-to-designer message. Output includes Build-tightness line. Commit 032ec98a. CLOSES D-scaffold-not-correct-2026-04-21 (4/4).
body.description:
Add a build-tightness score to /review filing-time output. Rubric: could a junior engineer (or a cheaper LLM) execute this ticket without making judgment calls about design? Scoring factors: are affected files named, is scope boundary explicit, are design rules addressed, is test plan specific. Output a grade (tight / medium / loose).

Low grade -> AMEND with guidance: 'this ticket makes the builder do design work; bounce back to the designer to tighten before filing'. Medium -> advisory note. Tight -> PASS.

Scope boundary: scoring logic + threshold + AMEND reason text. No UI changes beyond /review output.

Why: the leverage point Akien identified — well-speced tickets make cheap models viable as builders, and the bar for 'well-speced' should be explicit.

Gated on T-review-loads-palace-rules.

test_plan: feed a vague ticket ('make it better'), confirm loose grade; feed a tight one, confirm tight grade.
